docs.title: hodlbook
docs.text:
A crypto paper-trading portfolio ledger API — built on pydynantic single-table DynamoDB.

hodlbook lets users run a virtual crypto portfolio: hold a cash balance, place buy/sell orders at live (or mocked) prices, and track holdings, valuation, and a full trade history — with no real money. It exists to be a real, working application of pydynantic: every access pattern is a single-table design, trades are atomic DynamoDB transactions, balances use optimistic locking, prices are TTL-cached, and history is cursor-paginated.

Status: alpha / under construction. See ROADMAP.md for the plan and progress to a 1.0 release.

docs.title: Why it exists
docs.text:
This is a deliberate dogfooding project for pydynantic. It exercises the whole library surface against a realistic domain:

Domain need pydynantic feature One table, many entities single-table modelling + __entity__ discrimination "trades for a portfolio", "trades by symbol" primary key + GSIs Atomic buy/sell (cash ↓, holding ↑, trade recorded) transaction(...) No double-spend on balance optimistic locking (version_attr) Price cache that expires ttl_attr Created/updated bookkeeping created_at_attr / updated_at_attr Trade history pages cursor pagination (.page(cursor=...)) Cost/latency tracing observability hooks (on_operation)

If the app surfaces a gap in pydynantic, the fix lands upstream in the library.

docs.title: Stack
docs.text:
• Python 3.10+, FastAPI for the HTTP API.
  • pydynantic for the data layer over DynamoDB (the boto3 client is injected — DynamoDB Local or AWS in prod, moto in tests).
  • pytest + moto for tests; ruff + mypy --strict for quality.

docs.title: Usage
docs.text:
hodlbook runs against any DynamoDB endpoint via an injected boto3 client (DynamoDB Local / AWS in prod, moto in tests). The core flow:

import boto3
from decimal import Decimal
from hodlbook import (
    build_table, create_table, Repository, TradingEngine,
    MockPriceProvider, PriceCache, Valuator, AlertEvaluator, Side, Direction,
)

client = boto3.client("dynamodb")          # injected — your creds/region/endpoint
create_table(client)                       # one-time (or use CDK/Terraform in prod)

repo   = Repository(build_table(client))
prices = PriceCache(repo, MockPriceProvider({"bitcoin": Decimal("50000")}))
engine = TradingEngine(repo)
valuator = Valuator(repo, prices)

repo.create_portfolio(user_id="u1", portfolio_id="main", cash=Decimal("100000"))

# Atomic buy: cash debited, holding upserted, trade recorded — all-or-nothing.
engine.buy("u1", "main", "bitcoin", Decimal("1"), Decimal("50000"))

result = engine.sell("u1", "main", "bitcoin", Decimal("0.5"), Decimal("60000"))
print(result.realized_pnl)                 # Decimal('5000')

snapshot = valuator.value("u1", "main")    # cash + holdings, marked to current prices
print(snapshot.total_value, snapshot.total_unrealized_pnl)

# Price alerts (fired by the evaluator against fresh prices).
repo.create_alert("main", "a1", "bitcoin", Direction.ABOVE, Decimal("65000"))
fired = AlertEvaluator(repo, prices).evaluate(["bitcoin"])

docs.title: HTTP API (FastAPI)
docs.text:
import boto3
from hodlbook import create_app

app = create_app(boto3.client("dynamodb"))   # inject the client; ASGI app
# uvicorn:  uvicorn mymodule:app
# Endpoints: POST /portfolios, POST /portfolios/{user}/{pf}/orders,
#            GET .../holdings, .../valuation, .../trades?cursor=, alerts CRUD,
#            GET /prices/{symbol}

A complete, runnable end-to-end example (no AWS needed — uses moto) lives in examples/demo.py.

docs.title: Development
docs.text:
pip install -e ".[dev]"
pytest
ruff check .
mypy

docs.title: API Reference
docs.text: This page is generated from the source by mkdocstrings. It documents every symbol exported from the top-level hodlbook package (its __all__).

docs.title: hodlbook
docs.text: hodlbook: a crypto paper-trading portfolio ledger built on pydynantic.

docs.title: AlertEvaluator
docs.text: Evaluates armed alerts against current prices and fires the crossers.

docs.title: evaluate(symbols)
docs.text: Fire every armed alert on symbols whose threshold the price crosses.

docs.title: Analytics
docs.text: Snapshotting, returns, and leaderboard ranking over a portfolio's value.

docs.title: leaderboard(limit)
docs.text: The top-limit portfolios ranked by total value, highest first.

docs.title: returns(portfolio_id)
docs.text:
Percent change of total value from the first to the latest snapshot.

Returns Decimal("0") when fewer than two snapshots exist or the first value is zero (no baseline to measure against).

docs.title: series(portfolio_id, *, cursor=None, limit=None)
docs.text: A most-recent-first page of value snapshots for a portfolio.

docs.title: take_snapshot(user_id, portfolio_id)
docs.text:
Mark to market, persist a :class:Snapshot, and refresh the leaderboard.

Raises :class:~hodlbook.errors.InvalidOrder (-> 422) if the portfolio does not exist -- the valuator does the existence check.

docs.title: AuthenticationError
docs.text:
Bases: HodlbookError

The request carried no valid API key (missing, unknown, or revoked).

docs.title: AuthorizationError
docs.text:
Bases: HodlbookError

An authenticated caller tried to access another principal's resources.

docs.title: Direction
docs.text:
Bases: str, Enum

Whether a price alert fires above or below its threshold.

docs.title: ExecutionResult dataclass
docs.text: Outcome of one :meth:OrderExecutor.execute pass.

docs.title: FilledOrder dataclass
docs.text: An order that filled, paired with the market price it filled at.

docs.title: FiredAlert dataclass
docs.text: An alert that fired, paired with the price that crossed its threshold.

docs.title: HodlbookError
docs.text:
Bases: Exception

Base class for every error raised by the hodlbook trading engine.

docs.title: HoldingValuation dataclass
docs.text: A single holding marked to market.

docs.title: HttpPriceProvider
docs.text: Thin CoinGecko client. Network access only through the injected client.

docs.title: InsufficientFunds
docs.text:
Bases: HodlbookError

A buy was rejected because the portfolio lacks enough cash.

docs.title: InsufficientHoldings
docs.text:
Bases: HodlbookError

A sell was rejected because the portfolio lacks enough of the symbol.

docs.title: InvalidOrder
docs.text:
Bases: HodlbookError

An order was malformed (bad quantity/price, or unknown portfolio).

docs.title: MockPriceProvider
docs.text:
A deterministic provider for tests and offline runs.

Prices drift purely as a function of (symbol, drift_step) -- no random, no wall clock -- so a given step always yields the same price.

docs.title: Models dataclass
docs.text: Container of the five entity classes bound to one table.

docs.title: OrderExecutor
docs.text: Evaluates OPEN advanced orders against current prices and fills them.

docs.title: execute(symbols)
docs.text: Fill every OPEN order on symbols whose trigger the price has met.

docs.title: OrderNotFound
docs.text:
Bases: HodlbookError

An advanced order was requested by an id that does not exist.

docs.title: OrderStatus
docs.text:
Bases: str, Enum

Lifecycle state of an advanced order.

docs.title: OrderType
docs.text:
Bases: str, Enum

The kind of an advanced order.

docs.title: PriceCache
docs.text: A TTL cache over :class:PriceTick, fronting any :class:PriceProvider.

docs.title: PriceProvider
docs.text:
Bases: Protocol

A source of current USD prices keyed by symbol.

Single method by design: a Protocol with only one required member avoids the default-method friction mypy raises when a Protocol mixes concrete and abstract members.

docs.title: RateLimitExceeded
docs.text:
Bases: HodlbookError

A principal exceeded the per-minute request quota (-> HTTP 429).

docs.title: Repository
docs.text: Persistence helpers for hodlbook entities, bound to one table.

docs.title: advance_dca(order)
docs.text:
Persist a DCA order's advanced schedule, guarded on status == OPEN.

Mirrors :meth:mark_order_filled -- the optimistic version lock plus the status == OPEN guard make a lost race a no-op. The caller mutates next_run / remaining_runs / status before calling.

docs.title: cancel_order(portfolio_id, order_id)
docs.text:
Cancel an OPEN order, guarded so a cancel/fill race is safe.

Guarded with F("status") == OrderStatus.OPEN: if the order has already been filled or cancelled, the conditional put raises :class:pydynantic.ConditionCheckFailedError, which we swallow and treat as already-resolved (the cancel is a no-op).

docs.title: create_order(portfolio_id, order_id, user_id, symbol, side, order_type, quantity, *, limit_price=None, interval_seconds=None, next_run=None, remaining_runs=None)
docs.text: Create an OPEN order, failing if one already exists at that key.

docs.title: create_portfolio(user_id, portfolio_id, cash=Decimal('0'))
docs.text: Create a portfolio, failing if one already exists at that key.

docs.title: delete_alert(portfolio_id, alert_id)
docs.text: Delete an alert by its primary key (idempotent).

docs.title: get_holdings(portfolio_id)
docs.text: All holdings for a portfolio (mirrors :meth:list_alerts).

docs.title: issue_api_key(user_id)
docs.text:
Mint a new API key for user_id.

Returns (raw_token, entity). The raw token is generated here and returned ONLY from this method -- the table stores its SHA-256 hash, so the plaintext cannot be recovered afterwards.

docs.title: list_open_orders_by_symbol(symbol)
docs.text: All OPEN orders for symbol across portfolios (GSI2).

docs.title: list_snapshots(portfolio_id, *, cursor=None, limit=None)
docs.text: Most-recent-first page of value snapshots for a portfolio.

docs.title: list_trades(portfolio_id, *, cursor=None, limit=None)
docs.text: Most-recent-first page of trades for a portfolio.

docs.title: list_trades_by_symbol(symbol, *, cursor=None)
docs.text: Most-recent-first page of trades for a symbol across portfolios (GSI1).

docs.title: mark_alert_triggered(portfolio_id, alert_id)
docs.text:
Mark an alert triggered, conditionally so a double-fire is a no-op.

Guarded with F("triggered") == False so a re-fire / lost race raises :class:pydynantic.ConditionCheckFailedError rather than silently re-stamping an already-triggered alert.

docs.title: mark_order_filled(order)
docs.text:
Mark an order FILLED, guarded on status == OPEN AND version.

The version lock is applied automatically by put from the loaded order's version; we add F("status") == OPEN so a concurrent fill/cancel makes this a safe no-op. Raises :class:pydynantic.ConditionCheckFailedError on a lost race.

docs.title: revoke_api_key(user_id, key_id)
docs.text: Mark a user's API key revoked (no-op if it does not exist).

docs.title: top_leaderboard(limit)
docs.text: Top-limit leaderboard entries by total value, highest first (GSI1).

docs.title: upsert_leaderboard_entry(user_id, portfolio_id, total_value, rank_key, taken_at)
docs.text:
Insert-or-replace a portfolio's leaderboard entry at its primary key.

The primary key is stable per (user_id, portfolio_id) so re-snapshotting overwrites the prior entry rather than accumulating; rank_key (the zero-padded value) rides along on GSI1 for ranked reads.

docs.title: Settings
docs.text:
Bases: BaseSettings

Process configuration, populated from HODLBOOK_* env vars / .env.

Fields

table_name: DynamoDB single-table name. aws_region: AWS region for the DynamoDB client. dynamodb_endpoint: Optional endpoint override (e.g. DynamoDB Local). price_provider: Price source -- "mock" or "http". price_ttl_seconds: Freshness window for cached price ticks. default_starting_cash: Default cash balance for new portfolios. log_level: Root log level (e.g. "INFO", "DEBUG"). rate_limit_per_minute: Max requests per principal per fixed minute window. metrics_enabled: Whether to record/serve Prometheus metrics.

docs.title: Side
docs.text:
Bases: str, Enum

Direction of a trade.

docs.title: SkippedOrder dataclass
docs.text: An order whose fill the engine rejected (insufficient funds/holdings).

docs.title: TradeConflict
docs.text:
Bases: HodlbookError

A trade lost the optimistic-lock race repeatedly and gave up retrying.

docs.title: TradeResult dataclass
docs.text:
Outcome of a trade: the persisted trade plus realized P&L.

realized_pnl is always Decimal("0") for buys (cost basis only moves on a sell) and (price - avg_cost) * quantity for sells. The Trade schema intentionally carries no P&L field; it is surfaced here instead.

docs.title: TradingEngine
docs.text: Executes atomic buy/sell orders against a :class:Repository.

docs.title: buy(user_id, portfolio_id, symbol, quantity, price)
docs.text: Buy quantity of symbol at price, atomically.

docs.title: sell(user_id, portfolio_id, symbol, quantity, price)
docs.text: Sell quantity of symbol at price, atomically.

docs.title: UnknownSymbol
docs.text:
Bases: HodlbookError

A price was requested for a symbol the price provider does not know.

docs.title: Valuation dataclass
docs.text: A whole-portfolio snapshot: cash plus marked-to-market holdings.

docs.title: Valuator
docs.text: Computes a mark-to-market :class:Valuation for a portfolio.

docs.title: build_models(table)
docs.text:
Define the five entity classes bound to table and return :class:Models.

Defining the classes here (rather than at module scope) keeps the injected client isolated per table -- the same factory approach pydynantic uses in its own test suite.

docs.title: build_table(client, *, on_operation=None)
docs.text:
Build the hodlbook :class:~pydynantic.Table bound to client.

Pass on_operation to opt into pydynantic's observability hook -- it fires once per DynamoDB call for tracing and cost attribution. The default of None leaves behavior unchanged.

docs.title: collecting_hook()
docs.text:
Build a hook that appends every event to a list, returned alongside it.

Useful for tests and ad-hoc metrics collection::

hook, events = collecting_hook()
table = build_table(client, on_operation=hook)
...  # events now holds one OperationEvent per DynamoDB call

docs.title: create_app(client, *, provider=None, clock=None, on_operation=None, settings=None)
docs.text:
Build the hodlbook FastAPI app from an injected boto3 client.

Services are constructed once here (never boto3 itself) and stored on app.state; the Depends accessors read them back per request.

Pass on_operation (e.g. observability.logging_hook()) to enable optional tracing/cost-attribution logging around each DynamoDB call. The default of None leaves behavior unchanged.

Pass settings to override the env-driven configuration (defaults to :func:~hodlbook.settings.get_settings); it drives values like the price cache TTL while leaving every other parameter backward-compatible.

docs.title: create_table(client)
docs.text:
Issue a boto3 create_table for the hodlbook single table.

PK/SK plus two GSIs (GSI1, GSI2), all attributes S, projection ALL, on-demand billing. Mirrors pydynantic's tests/conftest.py.

docs.title: get_settings() cached
docs.text:
Return the process-wide :class:Settings, parsed once and cached.

Call get_settings.cache_clear() after mutating the environment (e.g. in tests) to force a fresh read.

docs.title: logging_hook(logger=None)
docs.text:
Build an :data:~pydynantic.OperationHook that logs each operation.

Each :class:~pydynantic.OperationEvent is logged at DEBUG on success and at INFO on failure (the exception name is included). Pass a custom logger to route the records; defaults to logging.getLogger( "hodlbook.dynamodb").

docs.title: setup_logging(level)
docs.text:
Configure the root logger with a single JSON-emitting stream handler.

Idempotent across calls: any handler previously installed by this function is removed first so repeated create_app calls (e.g. in tests) do not stack handlers. level is a stdlib level name such as "INFO".

docs.title: create_app(client, *, provider=None, clock=None, on_operation=None, settings=None)
docs.text:
Build the hodlbook FastAPI app from an injected boto3 client.

Services are constructed once here (never boto3 itself) and stored on app.state; the Depends accessors read them back per request.

Pass on_operation (e.g. observability.logging_hook()) to enable optional tracing/cost-attribution logging around each DynamoDB call. The default of None leaves behavior unchanged.

Pass settings to override the env-driven configuration (defaults to :func:~hodlbook.settings.get_settings); it drives values like the price cache TTL while leaving every other parameter backward-compatible.

docs.text:
Build a hook that appends every event to a list, returned alongside it.

Useful for tests and ad-hoc metrics collection::

hook, events = collecting_hook()
table = build_table(client, on_operation=hook)
...  # events now holds one OperationEvent per DynamoDB call

Each :class:~pydynantic.OperationEvent is logged at DEBUG on success and at INFO on failure (the exception name is included). Pass a custom logger to route the records; defaults to logging.getLogger( "hodlbook.dynamodb").

Idempotent across calls: any handler previously installed by this function is removed first so repeated create_app calls (e.g. in tests) do not stack handlers. level is a stdlib level name such as "INFO".

Defining the classes here (rather than at module scope) keeps the injected client isolated per table -- the same factory approach pydynantic uses in its own test suite.

Pass on_operation to opt into pydynantic's observability hook -- it fires once per DynamoDB call for tracing and cost attribution. The default of None leaves behavior unchanged.

docs.title: create_table(client)
docs.text:
Issue a boto3 create_table for the hodlbook single table.

PK/SK plus two GSIs (GSI1, GSI2), all attributes S, projection ALL, on-demand billing. Mirrors pydynantic's tests/conftest.py.

docs.title: Concepts
docs.text: hodlbook is a deliberate, end-to-end application of single-table design on DynamoDB, expressed entirely through pydynantic. This page explains the data model and the principles behind it.

docs.title: Guiding principles
docs.text:
• Single-table first — one DynamoDB table, many entities, access patterns designed up front and expressed as pydynantic keys/GSIs.
  • Money is never wrong — balances and holdings change only inside atomic transactions; concurrent writes are guarded by optimistic locking. No double-spend, no partial trades.
  • Inject the client — the boto3 client is injected everywhere (DynamoDB Local / AWS in prod, moto in tests). No hidden global state.
  • Typed end to end — mypy --strict clean, ruff clean, tests on moto.
  • Fix the library upstream — if hodlbook needs something pydynantic can't do, the fix lands in pydynantic; the behavior is never forked into the app.

docs.title: Single-table design
docs.text:
A single DynamoDB table (hodlbook) holds every entity, distinguished by an __entity__ discriminator. The table has a primary key (PK/SK) plus two global secondary indexes, GSI1 and GSI2. Each entity declares its own key templates and which GSIs it projects onto, so the access patterns are designed up front rather than discovered at query time.

The table name is exported as TABLE_NAME. The table and its entity classes are built from an injected client by build_table / build_models; create_table provisions it once.

docs.title: Domain model
docs.text:
One table hodlbook (PK/SK + GSI1, GSI2). Entities:

Entity PK SK Notes Portfolio USER#{user_id} PORTFOLIO#{portfolio_id} holds cash balance + a version for optimistic locking Holding PORTFOLIO#{portfolio_id} HOLDING#{symbol} a position in one asset: quantity, avg_cost Trade PORTFOLIO#{portfolio_id} TRADE#{ts}#{trade_id} an executed buy/sell; GSI1 by_symbol (SYMBOL#{symbol} / TRADE#{ts}) PriceTick PRICE#{symbol} TICK TTL-cached spot price; uses ttl_attr Alert PORTFOLIO#{portfolio_id} ALERT#{alert_id} a price threshold; GSI2 by_symbol for the evaluator

These five entities are defined together inside build_models, so each injected client gets its own isolated set of classes.

docs.title: Money is never wrong
docs.text:
Two pydynantic features keep balances correct under concurrency:

  • Atomic transactions — a buy or sell mutates the portfolio's cash, the per-symbol holding, and appends an immutable trade record in a single DynamoDB transaction. Either every change lands or none does.
  • Optimistic locking — each Portfolio carries a version. A trade reads the version, guards the transaction with a condition on it, and bumps it. A lost race cancels the transaction; the TradingEngine retries up to MAX_RETRIES times before raising TradeConflict.

All monetary math uses decimal.Decimal — float never touches money.

docs.title: Prices and TTL caching
docs.text:
A PriceProvider is the source of truth for a symbol's current price. Two implementations ship: MockPriceProvider (deterministic, for tests and offline use) and HttpPriceProvider (a thin CoinGecko client over an injected httpx.Client).

PriceCache sits in front of any provider, persisting fetched prices as PriceTick rows (using pydynantic's ttl_attr) and serving them until they expire — with an injected clock so freshness is testable.

docs.title: Errors
docs.text: The domain error hierarchy is rooted at HodlbookError and is independent of pydynantic's exceptions. The engine catches pydynantic's transaction/condition failures internally (to drive optimistic-lock retries) and translates them into domain errors — InsufficientFunds, InsufficientHoldings, InvalidOrder, TradeConflict, UnknownSymbol — so storage-layer concerns never leak out.

docs.title: Deployment
docs.text: hodlbook ships as a slim, non-root container plus a hodlbook admin CLI. The API never constructs a boto3 client itself: the client is resolved from the environment, so the same image runs against DynamoDB Local or AWS.

docs.title: Environment variables
docs.text:
Variable Purpose Example HODLBOOK_DYNAMODB_ENDPOINT DynamoDB endpoint URL. Omit to target real AWS. http://dynamodb:8000 AWS_REGION AWS region (falls back to AWS_DEFAULT_REGION). us-east-1 AWS_DEFAULT_REGION Region fallback when AWS_REGION is unset. us-east-1 AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID AWS credentials. Use dummy values for DynamoDB Local. local AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY AWS credentials. Use dummy values for DynamoDB Local. local

When no region is provided the CLI defaults to us-east-1.

docs.title: Local stack: docker compose up
docs.text:
docker-compose.yml defines two services:

  • dynamodb — amazon/dynamodb-local on port 8000.
  • api — built from Dockerfile, served by uvicorn --factory hodlbook.cli:app_factory, published on host port 8080, pointed at the dynamodb service via HODLBOOK_DYNAMODB_ENDPOINT with dummy local AWS creds.
docker compose up --build

The API comes up at http://localhost:8080. DynamoDB Local runs -inMemory, so its data is discarded when the container stops.

docs.title: Table provisioning
docs.text:
The image does not auto-create the table. Provision it once after the stack is up by running the CLI inside the api container (it inherits the same env):

docker compose exec api hodlbook create-table

create-table is idempotent — a second run reports the table already exists.

Optionally seed a demo portfolio with a couple of trades:

docker compose exec api hodlbook seed-demo --user-id demo --portfolio-id main --cash 100000

docs.title: CLI reference
docs.text:
The hodlbook console-script is installed with the package. Every subcommand accepts --region and --endpoint-url (both fall back to the environment):

Command Description create-table Provision the single hodlbook DynamoDB table (idempotent). seed-demo Create a demo portfolio plus two trades (bitcoin, ethereum). issue-api-key Placeholder — the API-key admin flow lands with M9 (auth). refresh-prices Placeholder — prices are refreshed lazily on read for now.

docs.title: Deploying to AWS
docs.text:
Run the same image with real AWS credentials and no HODLBOOK_DYNAMODB_ENDPOINT (so boto3 targets the real DynamoDB service), then provision the table once:

AWS_REGION=us-east-1 hodlbook create-table

docs.title: Guide
docs.text:
This walks through the core hodlbook flow using the real public API: wire the storage stack, open a portfolio, trade, value the book, page through history, and arm price alerts. Every name below is exported from hodlbook (see the API Reference).

All money is decimal.Decimal — never float.

docs.title: 1. Wire the stack
docs.text:
The boto3 client is injected everywhere. In production point it at AWS or DynamoDB Local; in tests wrap it in moto.

import boto3
from decimal import Decimal
from hodlbook import (
    build_table, create_table, Repository, TradingEngine,
    MockPriceProvider, PriceCache, Valuator, AlertEvaluator, Direction,
)

client = boto3.client("dynamodb")   # injected
create_table(client)                # one-time provisioning

repo = Repository(build_table(client))
prices = PriceCache(repo, MockPriceProvider({"bitcoin": Decimal("50000")}))
engine = TradingEngine(repo)
valuator = Valuator(repo, prices)
alerts = AlertEvaluator(repo, prices)

Repository is the typed storage layer; TradingEngine, Valuator, and AlertEvaluator are the business-logic services built on top of it.

docs.title: 2. Open a portfolio
docs.text:
repo.create_portfolio("u1", "main", cash=Decimal("100000"))

create_portfolio fails if a portfolio already exists at that key.

docs.title: 3. Place orders
docs.text:
buy and sell are atomic: cash, the per-symbol holding, and an immutable trade record all move together, guarded by optimistic locking.

# Buy 1 BTC at 50,000 — cash debited, holding upserted, trade recorded.
result = engine.buy("u1", "main", "bitcoin", Decimal("1"), Decimal("50000"))

# Sell half at a higher price — realized P&L is computed on the way out.
result = engine.sell("u1", "main", "bitcoin", Decimal("0.5"), Decimal("60000"))
print(result.realized_pnl)   # Decimal('5000')

Both return a TradeResult with the persisted trade and realized_pnl (always Decimal("0") for buys). Invalid orders raise typed errors — InvalidOrder for non-positive quantity/price, InsufficientFunds on a buy you can't afford, InsufficientHoldings on a sell larger than the position, and TradeConflict if optimistic-lock retries are exhausted.

docs.title: 4. Value the portfolio
docs.text:
Valuator.value marks every holding to its latest cached price and rolls cash + holdings into a Valuation snapshot.

snapshot = valuator.value("u1", "main")
print(snapshot.cash)                   # uninvested cash
print(snapshot.holdings_value)         # market value of all positions
print(snapshot.total_value)            # cash + holdings_value
print(snapshot.total_unrealized_pnl)   # open P&L across holdings

for h in snapshot.holdings:            # each is a HoldingValuation
    print(h.symbol, h.quantity, h.price, h.market_value, h.unrealized_pnl)

docs.title: 5. Page through trade history
docs.text:
Trades are cursor-paginated. Repository.list_trades returns a page; pass the previous page's cursor to fetch the next.

page = repo.list_trades("main", limit=50)
for trade in page.items:
    print(trade)

if page.cursor:                        # more pages remain
    nxt = repo.list_trades("main", cursor=page.cursor, limit=50)

docs.title: 6. Arm and fire price alerts
docs.text:
Create a threshold alert, then have the evaluator fire any that the current prices have crossed.

repo.create_alert("main", "a1", "bitcoin", Direction.ABOVE, Decimal("65000"))

fired = alerts.evaluate(["bitcoin"])   # list[FiredAlert]
for f in fired:
    print(f.alert, f.price)            # the alert and the price that crossed it

Firing is idempotent: a second pass over the same prices fires nothing. Direction.ABOVE fires when price >= threshold, Direction.BELOW when price <= threshold.

docs.title: HTTP API
docs.text:
The same flow is served over HTTP by a FastAPI app. create_app wires the whole stack from an injected client and returns an ASGI app:

import boto3
from hodlbook import create_app

app = create_app(boto3.client("dynamodb"))   # ASGI app
# uvicorn mymodule:app

Endpoints cover the flow above: create portfolios, place orders, list holdings, portfolio valuation, cursor-paginated trade history, alert CRUD, and current prices. Domain and pydynantic exceptions are translated to a uniform {"error", "detail"} JSON body by centralized handlers.

docs.title: Observability
docs.text: Wire pydynantic's on_operation hook to opt into tracing without forcing a logging dependency. logging_hook logs each DynamoDB operation; collecting_hook collects events for inspection (handy in tests). Pass one to build_table / create_app as the table's operation hook.
